methods.description: Analysis of diatom assemblage structure using species richness, Shannon-Wiener diversity index, and Pielou's evenness index to evaluate differences between islands and the mainland.

steps.description: The structure of diatom assemblages was examined by species richness (R), Shannon-Wiener diversity index (H') and Pielou's evenness index (J') calculated using OMNIDIA 6.0 software.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate species richness, Shannon-Wiener diversity index, and Pielou's evenness index using OMNIDIA 6.0

steps.description: Differences in structural indices between islands and the mainland were evaluated by a paired t-test and after verification of normal distribution with the Shapiro-Wilk test using R 3.6.1.
steps.searchable_summary: perform paired t-test and Shapiro-Wilk test using R

methods.description: Conversion of diatom data to relative abundances for statistical analyses, focusing on species with significant presence.

steps.description: All diatom data were converted in relative abundances (RA) for the statistical analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: convert diatom data to relative abundances

steps.description: To minimize the influence of rare species, only the species with RA ≥5% in at least two sites are included in the analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: filter species with RA ≥5% in at least two sites

methods.description: Attribution of ecological preferences to the most abundant diatom species based on pH, trophic state, salinity, and moisture.

steps.description: Ecological preferences of the most abundant diatom species (RA ≥5%) for pH, trophic state, salinity and moisture were attributed according to Van Dam et al.
steps.searchable_summary: assign ecological preferences to diatom species

steps.description: The taxa with a wider ecological range were equally placed among the respective autecological levels to obtain a simplified framework.
steps.searchable_summary: categorize taxa with wide ecological range

methods.description: Analysis of similarity among diatom assemblages using non-metric multidimensional scaling ordination and ANOSIM.

steps.description: The similarity among diatom assemblages of the four geographic regions was analysed using a non-metric multidimensional scaling ordination (nMDS).
steps.searchable_summary: perform nMDS on diatom assemblage data

steps.description: The ordination was performed on a Bray-Curtis similarity matrix of species data log (x+1) transformed.
steps.searchable_summary: create Bray-Curtis similarity matrix with log transformation

steps.description: The significance of the differences was tested by a one-way analysis of similarities (ANOSIM).
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOSIM to test significance of differences

methods.description: Exploration of relationships between diatom species and environmental variables using Canonical Correspondence Analysis (CCA).

steps.description: Relationships between diatom species and environmental variables were explored by a Canonical Correspondence Analysis (CCA) after the previous assessment of the length of the gradient (>4) by means of a Detrended Correspondence Analysis (DCA) of diatom data using Canoco 4.5.
steps.searchable_summary: perform CCA after DCA gradient assessment

steps.description: The two matrices, for physical and chemical data (except for pH), and for diatom data, were log (x+1) transformed.
steps.searchable_summary: apply log transformation to data matrices

steps.description: All canonical axes were used to assess the significant variables through analyses by means of a Monte Carlo test (499 permutations).
steps.searchable_summary: use Monte Carlo test to assess significant variables

methods.description: Identification of the most characteristic species of groups defined by CCA using the Indicator Value method.

steps.description: The indicator value method (IndVal) was used to identify the most characteristic species of three groups defined by the CCA.
steps.searchable_summary: apply IndVal to identify characteristic species

steps.description: Indicator values were calculated using abundance and frequency of occurrence of each species in each group as the input data.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate indicator values using abundance and frequency

steps.description: The species with p <0.05 based on 499 permutations from the Monte Carlo test were considered to be indicator species.
steps.searchable_summary: identify indicator species with p <0.05

methods.description: Collection of diatom and water samples, measurement of environmental parameters, and assessment of anthropogenic disturbances.

steps.description: Sampling was carried out once in the winter season between 2007 and 2017 (N = 23). The geographic position and altitude of the springs were recorded by a GPS.
steps.searchable_summary: record geographic position and altitude using GPS

steps.description: Water temperature, pH and conductivity were measured in situ using digital multi-parametric probes.
steps.searchable_summary: measure water temperature, pH, and conductivity

steps.description: Anthropogenic disturbances were evaluated in an ordinal scale with disturbance scores: 1 = low; 2 = medium; 3 = high.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate anthropogenic disturbances using ordinal scale

steps.description: Shading conditions and current velocity were assessed using a five-score scale.
steps.searchable_summary: assess shading conditions and current velocity

steps.description: In most springs, discharge was measured by the volumetric method using a graduated bucket and a chronometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure discharge using volumetric method

steps.description: Diatom and water samples for physical and chemical analyses were collected simultaneously.
steps.searchable_summary: collect diatom and water samples

steps.description: Diatoms were collected with a toothbrush from hard natural substrata (five stones) along a longitudinal transect from the emergence point up to a max distance of 5 m following the European Standard.
steps.searchable_summary: collect diatoms using toothbrush from substrata

steps.description: Water samples were collected at the emergence point using 1-L polypropylene and polyethylene bottles and were transported in cold and dark conditions for the laboratory analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and transport water samples for analysis
